segments.text: A warning about being reproved
He that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: He…: Heb. A man of reproofs

segments.text: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth his substance.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The king by judgment establisheth the land: but he that receiveth gifts overthroweth it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: he…: Heb. a man of oblations

segments.text: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Scornful men bring a city into a snare: but wise men turn away wrath.
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: bring…: or, set a city on fire

segments.text: If a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, there is no rest.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The bloodthirsty hate the upright: but the just seek his soul.
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: The bloodthirsty: Heb. Men of blood

segments.text: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If a ruler hearken to lies, all his servants are wicked.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The LORD's interaction with people
The poor and the deceitful man meet together: the LORD lighteneth both their eyes.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: A servant will not be corrected by words: for though he understand he will not answer.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: An angry man stirreth up strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: A man's pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth cursing, and bewrayeth it not.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Many seek the ruler's favour; but every man's judgment cometh from the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: the ruler's…: Heb. the face of a ruler

segments.text: An unjust man is an abomination to the just: and he that is upright in the way is abomination to the wicked.
segments.speaker: Unknown
